retry_reason: The refcount_set operation is unconditional and never fails; the only potential failure is memory allocation (kzalloc), which is transient but the refcount wrapper itself does not govern retry behavior.
contract_summary: Set-wrapper: allocates a new ifacaddr6 object and sets its reference count to 1 (initial reference). The caller receives ownership of the object with one reference and must release it via aca_put (or similar) when done. If allocation fails, returns NULL; the caller must check the return value. The object's aca_rt field also holds a reference on the associated fib6_info via fib6_info_hold, which will be released by the corresponding put on aca.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate a new ifacaddr6 object and initialize its reference count to 1 via refcount_set, establishing the initial reference. The additional field assignments (address, timestamps, users) are standard initialization of the object's attributes.

check.reason: refcount_set on aca->aca_refcnt is correctly captured as 'set' on return variable aca, with access path aca.aca_refcnt.refs and type chain ifacaddr6--refcount_t--atomic_t. fib6_info_hold(f6i) increments the refcount on the input parameter f6i, captured as 'get' on parameter 1 with access path f6i.fib6_ref.refs and type chain fib6_info--refcount_t--atomic_t. Both operations are fully consistent with the code.
